raw_response:
```json
{
  "summary": "The technician performed a comprehensive system diagnostic and information gathering session on a Windows 10 Pro machine. This included reviewing system information, hardware, installed programs, event logs, user accounts, network configuration, and checking for Windows updates to identify potential issues and gather data for further troubleshooting. The session concluded with a restart of the machine.",
  "session_type": "troubleshooting",
  "actions_taken": [
    "Logged into the Windows 10 Pro machine as 'Administrator' (after one incorrect password attempt).",
    "Reviewed system properties, including OS version (Windows 10 Pro, 21H2, build 19044.1586), processor (Intel Xeon E5-2660 v3), and installed RAM (32 GB).",
    "Checked Disk Management for disk configuration.",
    "Examined Device Manager, specifically 'Display adapters' (noted 'Microsoft Basic Display Adapter'), 'Network adapters', and 'Processors'.",
    "Reviewed System Configuration (msconfig) for boot, services, and startup items.",
    "Opened and reviewed Task Manager tabs: Processes, Performance (CPU, Memory, Disk, Ethernet, GPU), Startup, Users, Details, and Services.",
    "Checked Display settings, noting the low desktop resolution (1024 x 768).",
    "Navigated to Control Panel > Programs and Features to list installed applications (e.g., VMware Tools, Plex Media Server, NVIDIA Graphics Driver).",
    "Used Computer Management to review Event Viewer logs (System, Application, Security, Setup), noting various warnings (iScsiPrt) and errors (Service Control Manager, DistributedCOM, Application Error for nvcontainer.exe, explorer.exe).",
    "Examined Local Users and Groups in Computer Management, checking 'Administrator' account properties.",
    "Verified Windows Update status ('You're up to date') and reviewed update history (Quality and Driver updates, including NVIDIA Display driver).",
    "Checked Network Connections (ncpa.cpl) for adapter status and details (IPv4 address, DNS servers, speed).",
    "Executed PowerShell commands: `ipconfig /all`, `ping google.com`, and `netstat -ano` to gather network information.",
    "Restarted the computer."
  ],
  "applications_used": [
    "Windows Lock Screen",
    "Settings",
    "Disk Management",
    "Device Manager",
    "System Configuration (msconfig)",
    "Task Manager",
    "Control Panel",
    "Programs and Features",
    "Computer Management",
    "Event Viewer",
    "Local Users and Groups",
    "Network Connections (ncpa.cpl)",
    "PowerShell",
    "UAC prompt"
  ],
  "issues_observed": [
    "Initial login attempt failed due to an incorrect password.",
    "System is using a 'Microsoft Basic Display Adapter' with a low resolution (1024x768), suggesting a missing or generic graphics driver.",
    "Event Viewer showed multiple errors and warnings, including: iScsiPrt (Event ID 129), Service Control Manager (Event ID 7000), DistributedCOM (Event ID 10005), and Application Errors (Event ID 1000) for nvcontainer.exe and explorer.exe."
  ],
  "issues_resolved": [],
  "machines_touched": [
    "DESKTOP-R..." (partial device name visible in System properties)
  ],
  "notable_events": [
    "The presence of a 'Microsoft Basic Display Adapter' and low screen resolution is a key observation, potentially indicating a graphics driver issue despite a NVIDIA driver being listed in update history.",
    "Numerous system and application errors were found in the Event Viewer, pointing to potential stability or configuration issues."
  ],
  "concerns": [],
  "estimated_productivity": "high",
  "tech_performance": {
    "appeared_stuck": "false",
    "stuck_details": [],
    "idle_gaps": [],
    "idle_gap_count": 0,
    "longest_idle_seconds": 0,
    "total_idle_estimate_seconds": 0,
    "efficiency_notes": "The technician worked methodically, moving efficiently between various diagnostic tools and system information screens without hesitation or repetitive actions. The information gathering process was well-structured and comprehensive.",
    "coaching_flags": []
  }
}
```

This included reviewing system information, hardware, installed programs, event logs, user accounts, network configuration, and checking for Windows updates to identify potential issues and gather data for further troubleshooting.
